title: How to File ITR — Income Tax Return Guide 2026
meta_description: How to file ITR online 2026 — step by step guide for salaried, self-employed, and freelancers. ITR-1, ITR-2, ITR-4 forms, deadline July 31, e-verification.

hero.one_liner: Complete step-by-step guide to filing your Income Tax Return online on the e-filing portal — with form selection, deductions, and deadline reminders

sections.heading: Overview
content: Filing an Income Tax Return (ITR) is a legal obligation for every Indian whose gross income exceeds the basic exemption limit (₹3 lakh under Old Regime, ₹3 lakh under New Regime for FY 2025-26). Even if your income is below the limit, filing ITR is highly recommended — it serves as income proof for home loans, car loans, credit card approvals, visa applications, and rental agreements.

There are 7 different ITR forms for different income types: ITR-1 (Sahaj) for salaried individuals with income up to ₹50 lakh and simple sources, ITR-2 for capital gains/multiple properties, ITR-3 for business income, ITR-4 (Sugam) for presumptive income (self-employed with turnover ≤₹1Cr), ITR-5/6/7 for firms/partnerships/companies. Most salaried employees use ITR-1 or ITR-2.

The income tax e-filing portal (incometax.gov.in) allows free online filing. Most of your data is pre-filled from Form 16 (from employer), Form 26AS (showing all TDS), and AIS (Annual Information Statement showing all income reported by others — banks, employers, investment platforms).
For simple salaried cases, filing takes just 15-20 minutes. No need for paying any charges or using paid software.

The deadline for individuals is July 31 of the assessment year (for income earned in the previous financial year). Filing ITR before July 31 is advisable — not only to avoid penalty but to claim refunds faster.
Filing after July 31 attracts a penalty of ₹5,000 (reduced to ₹1,000 if total income below ₹5 lakh). You also lose the ability to carry forward certain losses if filed late.

rows.label: Deadline for individuals
rows.value: July 31, 2026 (for FY 2025-26 / AY 2026-27). Filing after this deadline = ₹5,000 penalty (₹1,000 if income <₹5L).

rows.label: Portal
rows.value: incometax.gov.in — free filing, no charges, no software needed. Direct filing or via authorized e-filing agent.

rows.label: Who must file
rows.value: Anyone with gross income above ₹3 lakh in a financial year (before deductions) is obligated to file. Below ₹3L but received notice? You must file.

rows.label: ITR-1 form
rows.value: For salaried individuals with income up to ₹50 lakh, one house property, other income from interest/dividend/sports. Most common form used by 80% of taxpayers.

rows.label: ITR-4 form
rows.value: For freelancers/self-employed under presumptive income scheme (Section 44AD/44ADA). Used by doctors, consultants, content creators with turnover ≤₹1Cr.

rows.label: ITR-2 form
rows.value: For capital gains (stock/mutual fund sales, property sales), multiple house properties, or income requiring ITR-2 specifically.

rows.label: E-Verification
rows.value: MANDATORY. After submitting ITR, you must e-verify within 30 days using Aadhaar OTP (simplest), Net Banking, DSC, or post signed form. Without verification, ITR is considered NOT FILED.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step: Filing ITR-1 Online (for Most Salaried Employees)

steps.title: Login to incometax.gov.in
steps.description: Visit incometax.gov.in → Login with PAN number as User ID and your password. If first time, register using PAN, Aadhaar, and mobile number. Complete profile setup including email and contact verification.

steps.title: Navigate to File ITR
steps.description: Go to Dashboard → e-File → Income Tax Returns → 'File ITR' → Select Assessment Year 2026-27 (for income earned in FY 2025-26). Select ITR-1 (if you're salaried with income ≤₹50L, one house property, no capital gains). Select 'Online' filing mode.

steps.title: Choose tax regime — Old vs New (critical choice)
steps.description: The portal asks: Old Regime (allows deductions: 80C, 80D, HRA, etc.) or New Regime (lower tax rates but almost no deductions)? Check: If total deductions >₹3-4L, Old Regime saves more. If deductions <₹3L, New Regime saves more. The portal shows a comparison after you enter details. Most salaried employees with home loan benefit from Old Regime.

steps.title: Review and correct pre-filled data
steps.description: The portal auto-fills your salary from Form 16, TDS deducted, bank interest from Form 26AS, dividend income from AIS. VERIFY every number against your Form 16 and bank statements. Common errors: Bank interest shown but not claimed. Stock gains from past years shown. Dividend income shown. Edit any incorrect entries before proceeding.

steps.title: Enter all deductions (if Old Regime)
steps.description: Section 80C (up to ₹1.5L): PPF, ELSS, EPF, LIC, tax-saver FD, tuition fees, home loan principal. Section 80D: Health insurance premiums (₹25K for self + ₹25K for family, ₹50K for parents if senior citizens). Section 80CCD(1B): NPS (extra ₹50K). Section 24: Home loan interest (up to ₹2L). HRA exemption (if you receive HRA and paid rent). Enter investment amounts and you'll get year-end summaries.

steps.title: Review and calculate tax
steps.description: After entering deductions, the portal calculates your total tax liability, compares it with TDS already deducted from your salary, and shows: 'Tax Due' (you owe more tax) or 'Refund' (excess TDS will be returned to you). If tax is due, you must pay via Challan 280 before submitting ITR. Use net banking or UPI to pay.

steps.title: Submit ITR
steps.description: Review all details one final time. Click 'Submit'. A pop-up shows a summary of your ITR with total income, deductions, and tax. Verify and confirm submission. You'll receive an ITR-V (acknowledgment) with a unique number. Save this number.

steps.title: E-Verify IMMEDIATELY (most critical step)
steps.description: After submitting, you MUST e-verify within 30 days — this is MANDATORY. Options: (1) Aadhaar OTP (simplest — 6-digit OTP sent to Aadhaar-linked mobile). (2) Net Banking. (3) Digital Signature Certificate (DSC). (4) Post signed ITR-V form to CPC Bangalore. Without verification, your ITR is considered NOT FILED and you'll lose all tax benefits.

sections.heading: Old Regime vs New Regime — Which Saves More Tax?
rows: Depends on deductions (usually Old if deductions >₹2.5L)

sections.heading: Common Mistakes That Cost You Money
content:
Mistake 1: Not filing ITR because 'no tax is due'

Even with zero tax liability, file ITR — it serves as income proof for home loans (banks ask for last 2-3 years' ITR), car loans, credit card approvals (they verify ITR), visa applications, and rental agreements (landlords demand it). Missing ITR can cost you ₹20L+ home loan rejection or delayed approval.

Mistake 2: Not reporting bank interest and FD interest

The income tax department automatically gets this data from banks under Sec 26AS. If you don't report interest in your ITR but they have it in AIS (Annual Information Statement), you'll get a notice under Section 143(1) demanding tax + interest + penalty.
Banks report all FD/savings interest >₹40 to IT dept.

Mistake 3: Missing the e-Verification step

Submitting ITR is NOT enough — you MUST e-verify within 30 days. Many people submit ITR and forget to verify.
An unverified ITR is treated as if you never filed. You lose all deductions, refunds don't process, and the government can treat you as a non-filer.
Take 30 seconds to verify immediately after submission.

Mistake 4: Not claiming all eligible deductions

Common missed deductions: Section 80D (health insurance premium for self + parents — ₹25K+₹25K = ₹50K), Section 80TTA (₹10,000 savings interest deduction if you don't opt New Regime), NPS employer contribution (counts towards 80C), Education loan interest (Section 80E — no upper limit, only requirement: educational institution must be approved).

Mistake 5: Filing the wrong ITR form

ITR-1 is for salaried only (income ≤₹50L). If you have capital gains from stocks/MF/property sale, you need ITR-2.
If you have business income, you need ITR-3 or 4. Filing wrong form = notice from IT dept asking for correction = stress and delay.
Take 30 seconds to verify your form eligibility before filing.

Mistake 6: Wrong tax regime choice

Some people automatically choose New Regime without comparing. If your deductions total ₹5L (EPF ₹1L + 80C ₹1.5L + HRA ₹2.5L), Old Regime saves ₹1.5L+ in tax (30% of ₹5L).
New Regime might be worse for you. Always compare both before choosing.

steps.title: Collect all documents
steps.description: Form 16 (from employer — lists salary, TDS). Form 26AS (download from incometax portal — shows all TDS/income reported). Bank statements (for interest income). Investment proofs (PPF, ELSS, FD, LIC, tuition receipts). Home loan statement (principal paid). Health insurance proof.

steps.title: Login to incometax.gov.in
steps.description: Register if first-time, or login with PAN. Download Form 26AS and AIS from 'View Documents' to see all reported income and TDS. This helps you identify what needs to be reported in ITR.

steps.title: Choose form and regime
steps.description: Select ITR form (ITR-1 for salaried, ITR-4 for self-employed with presumptive income, ITR-2 for capital gains/multiple properties). Choose Old or New Regime.

steps.title: Fill details and verify pre-filled data
steps.description: Go through each section. Verify pre-filled data. Enter deductions, expenses, investment amounts. Check calculations at the end.

steps.title: Pay any tax due (if applicable)
steps.description: If 'Tax Due' shows, pay via Challan 280 before submitting. Use net banking, UPI, or in-person at bank.

steps.title: Submit and e-verify immediately
steps.description: Submit ITR. Within 30 days, e-verify using Aadhaar OTP (recommended) or other method. Save the ITR-V acknowledgment number. Mark in calendar: Download final ITR-V acknowledgment and file for records.

faqs.question: I earned less than ₹3 lakh this year. Do I need to file ITR?
faqs.answer: Legally, no — if your gross income is below the basic exemption limit (₹3L for both regimes), filing is not mandatory. However, it's highly recommended because: (1) If TDS was deducted on your income, you can claim refund by filing ITR. (2) ITR serves as income proof for loans and visa. (3) You can carry forward losses (if any) to offset against future income. Filing a 'Nil return' with zero income is free and takes 10 minutes — just do it.

faqs.question: What if I miss the July 31 deadline?
faqs.answer: You can still file a 'belated return' until December 31 of the assessment year (December 31, 2026 for FY 2025-26). Penalty: ₹5,000 (₹1,000 if income < ₹5L). Additionally: (1) You lose the right to carry forward certain losses. (2) Refund may take longer (3-6 months vs usual 15-45 days). (3) The government can impose interest if there's underpayment of tax. File on time to avoid all these complications.

faqs.question: I received a notice from the IT department. What do I do?
faqs.answer: Don't panic — most notices are auto-generated under Section 143(1) for minor mismatches (unreported bank interest, TDS differences, Form 26AS discrepancy). Login to the e-filing portal → 'Response to Outstanding Demand' → view the notice → respond within the given time. For simple mismatches, you can agree or disagree online. For complex notices (scrutiny under Section 143(2) with detailed inquiry), consult a CA. Respond within time limit to avoid penalty.

faqs.question: Can I file ITR myself or do I need a CA?
faqs.answer: For ITR-1 (salaried employees with income ≤₹50L, simple income sources), filing yourself is very easy — the portal auto-fills most data and has step-by-step guidance. For ITR-2 (capital gains), ITR-3/4 (business income), or complex situations (foreign income, multiple properties, losses), hiring a CA is recommended. CA fees range from ₹500-2,000 for ITR-1 and ₹2,000-5,000+ for complex returns. In metro cities, CAs charge higher fees.

faqs.question: How long does tax refund take after filing ITR?
faqs.answer: If you e-verify immediately after filing and your bank account is pre-validated on the portal: Refunds are typically processed within 15-45 days. Money is credited directly to your bank account linked with PAN. If it's been more than 60 days, check status at incometax.gov.in → 'View Refund/Demand Status'. If still pending, contact the assessing officer. Delays usually happen if: (1) Bank account details are wrong or not pre-validated, (2) Name mismatch between ITR and bank account, (3) High volume at the IT office.

disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only. Consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
